calibration.repoShape: Framework package source inside a larger repository with routing, runtime, build, and adapter surfaces
calibration.safetyPosture: Clone-only source scan; no target installs, package-manager scripts, target tests, target builds, submodules, or npx were run.
calibration.scopeQuestion: Can an inferred package-source baseline make framework package drift reviewable without scanning docs, examples, tests, or target installs?
calibration.commandSurface: axi infer --json + axi graph --json + axi diff + axi observe --baseline
calibration.mainSignal: The framework package source graph stayed drift-quiet; inference still preserved large-file pressure notes for Vite integration and type-generation surfaces.
calibration.decision: Do not change validator behavior; keep source/test scope explicit for framework package pilots.
calibration.codeChanged: No Axiom code changed; this is a no-install calibration run.
calibration.followUp: Compare with app-repo and generated-code-heavy smokes before changing scan defaults or advisory prominence.

reviewStory.summary: Starter contract inferred 1 module and 0 observed module edges from 193 source files.
reviewStory.setup: Scanned 193 source files and 540 imports; 1 candidate group became 1 starter module. This is a current-graph snapshot, not declared architecture intent yet.

pressures.title: Large-file pressure in inferred scope
pressures.description: 4 large source files may hide responsibilities that folder and import inference cannot split. Inspect these files before judging the starter module map as complete.

reviewStory.nextStep: Inspect large-file pressure notes, adjust module boundaries if needed, then run `axi observe --root . --spec <draft.axi> --markdown`.
reviewStory.caveat: Inference reads static imports and folder/package shape. It can lower authoring cost, but humans still decide module names, visibility, layers, accepted debt, and which edges are real architecture intent.
